home.headline: Two practices. One point of view.
home.introduction: I’m Charlie—a data scientist and active musician. I build useful systems and make music, often with the same instincts: listen closely, find the structure, and make it feel alive.

data.headline: Useful systems for messy questions.
data.introduction: I work across data science, analytics, machine learning, and software—turning ambiguity into tools people can understand and depend on.

cards.title: Code & projects
cards.body: Python, analysis, machine learning, and the infrastructure that helps good ideas hold up in the real world.

cards.title: Notes & experiments
cards.body: Technical write-ups, project notebooks, and occasional investigations into questions worth following.

music.headline: Music is a present-tense practice.
music.introduction: I’m a drummer, vibraphonist, songwriter, and arranger. Music isn’t a former chapter—it’s an active part of how I work, listen, and move through the world.
music.about: I studied jazz at NYU and co-founded Del Water Gap, spending six years recording and touring with the band. I continue to write, play, arrange, and follow the next good sound.

cards.title: Del Water Gap
cards.body: Recordings and live sessions from the band’s early years, with me behind the drums and elsewhere in the arrangements.

cards.title: Make something
cards.body: For playing, writing, arranging, recording, or simply talking music, I’m always happy to hear what you’re working on.
